items.title: V.E.L.O.C.I.T.Y.-OS: Reclaiming Ring 0 – UEFI Bootloader & GDT/IDT (Part 8)
items.summary: How I exited UEFI boot services to build a standalone, Ring 0 operating system kernel that runs JIT code in L3 cache.

items.title: V.E.L.O.C.I.T.Y.-OS: The x86-64 Machine-Code JIT & SCEV-Lite (Part 6)
items.summary: How I built a raw x86-64 machine-code compiler for scalar loops and implemented SCEV-Lite to solve loops at compile time.

items.title: V.E.L.O.C.I.T.Y.-OS: JIT Math Optimizations – Division-Free and In-Place (Part 5)
items.summary: How I optimized my JIT compiler arithmetic, replacing division and modulo instructions with lookup tables and bitwise operations.

items.title: V.E.L.O.C.I.T.Y.-OS: Swarms, Headless Streaming & RCU Hot-Patching (Part 11)
items.summary: How I built a multi-agent swarm runtime, streamed screen frames over serial, and hot-patched kernel drivers using RCU.
